message:
feat(ipc): tick 201 send_request_with_id_with_cx response read cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Mirror of tick 200 on the client side. IpcClient::send_request_with_id_with_cx
(ipc.rs L2029) already threaded cx through:
  - pre-start checkpoint
  - pre-connect checkpoint + compat_unix::connect (ambient await on
    connect; kept for now — server close is the only cancel here)
  - pre-write checkpoint
  - pre-read checkpoint

But the actual response read on L2121 used ambient
`compat_unix::next_line(&mut lines)` instead of
`next_line_with_cx(cx, &mut lines)`. The pre-read checkpoint gates
entry; the ambient read itself doesn't observe cx. If the server
stalls mid-response, the client hangs on the read until the stream
closes or the server eventually writes — even under an outer
cx-cancel.

Tick 201 switches L2121 to `next_line_with_cx(cx, ...)`. Client-side
cancellation now propagates into the response-line wait, matching
the server-side fix in tick 200 and the pattern in
native_events::handle_connection_with_cx.

Legacy send_request_with_id (non-cx path, L2138) unchanged.

Verification: 98 / 98 ipc:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target). The ipc_roundtrip,
ipc_pane_state_roundtrip, and user_var_event_reaches_event_bus
tests all exercise the client-side RPC path tick 201 fixed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
